families.hypothesis: With a pedestrian blocking the normal right escape, rightward T-drift should be vetoed; braking should dominate at longer TTC/lower ego speed, while the shortest-TTC/high-speed corner should expose whether a leftward evasive maneuver becomes preferable despite the crossing threat.

trace.analysis: Across the 17 lawful points in this family, full emergency braking (code 0) is a minimum-severity maneuver at 16 points. The only lawful non-braking-dominant point is the high-speed, short-TTC corner at ego_speed_mps=30.0, ttc_s=1.4, lateral_speed_mps=7.0, lateral_dist_m=9.8, where braking_sufficient=false and code 3 dominates; it has only one supporting lawful point, so no general rule is proposed for it. All lawful points satisfying braking_sufficient=true, pedestrian_side=right, corridor_right_free=false, on_collision_course=true, ttc_s in [1.4,2.4], lateral_speed_mps in [2.0,12.0], ego_speed_mps in [5.0,30.0], and the observed crossing-distance/risk ranges have code 0 among their minimum-severity maneuvers. TTC=0.4 cases are ambiguous/all-severe and are deliberately excluded. Existing overlapping rules are not directly contradicted by this grid because their pedestrian-side, approach-sector, or braking_sufficient guards exclude the contradictory cases.

families.hypothesis: With the right escape corridor blocked by a pedestrian at short TTC, the dominant maneuver should switch from right-drift escape to hard in-lane braking or left-behind-the-truck steering, with the boundary driven mainly by TTC, ego speed, and remaining right-side clearance.

trace.analysis: The lawful grid contains 3 points where maneuver 7 is among the minimum-severity maneuvers and selected as dominant: low/moderate ego speed with longer TTC (ttc_s 3.43-12.0, ego_speed_mps/closing_speed_mps 5.0-17.5). The only lawful braking-dominant points are 2 points, so a new braking rule would not satisfy the minimum 3-support requirement; existing braking rules are not contradicted by the lawful points because their overlapping lawful cases also allow/choose code 0. The safest supported generalization is therefore a no-intervention/monitoring rule for the intersection, front obstacle, right pedestrian/right corridor blocked case at sufficiently long TTC and ego speed no greater than 17.5 m/s.
